summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud config to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: The package impersonates the Anthropic ecosystem (name 'anthropic-toolkit', description 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK', @anthropic-ai/sdk declared as peer dependency, repository under an unrelated 'anthropic-toolkit' GitHub org). On npm install, scripts/postinstall.js harvests installer-side identity and cloud-config data and POSTs it to a hardcoded author endpoint at https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. Collected fields include: os.hostname(), os.userInfo() username, OS platform, current working directory, parent project package.json metadata, git user.email parsed from ~/.gitconfig and the consuming project's.git/config, the git remote origin URL, up to 15 recent committer emails from.git/logs/HEAD, GitHub CLI login and email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, comment fields from every ~/.ssh/*.pub key, GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, and /etc/resolv.conf search-domain. The 'diagnostics' framing is a cover story — the payload is bulk identity and cloud-targeting reconnaissance against the installer, with no opt-in and no relation to the package's advertised SDK-utility purpose. A self-imposed credential-pattern skip-list does not change that emails, GCP account, AWS profiles, and SSH key comments are sensitive identity material directly usable for follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
